title: AI Audit Trails: What to Log Before You Trust Analytics AI
summary: Regulators and customers ask how a number was produced. AI audit trails answer that for conversational analytics.

content.summary: Regulators and customers ask how a number was produced. AI audit trails answer that for conversational analytics.

body.text: A confident AI sentence is not lineage. Logs are. Record who uploaded the file, when, which filter rules were active, and which questions were asked. Store enough context to reproduce aggregates—not necessarily every token—so reviewers can rebuild charts from the same subset.

body.alt: Data visualization on a modern display
body.caption: Data visualization on a modern display. Photo: Unsplash

body.text: Record who uploaded the file, when, which filter rules were active, and which questions were asked.
Store enough context to reproduce aggregates—not necessarily every token—so reviewers can rebuild charts from the same subset.

items: Timestamp each Talk to Data session against file version.
Persist filter rule IDs with AI responses.
Export audit bundles for quarterly reviews.

body.text: Audit trails also help analysts debug their own mistakes—wrong rule, stale upload—without blaming the model.
Lightweight logging beats none for mid-market teams without enterprise data platforms.
Datilio's file workflow naturally anchors AI questions to uploads, filters, and timestamps you can trace.

meta_keywords: Audit Trail, AI Governance, Compliance, Logging
